comment_text.sn22.88:1.2: Not to be confused with the shameless Assaji of MN 70:4.1, this is probably the early disciple of that name (MN 35:3.1). Assaji was one the group of five mendicants who received the Buddha’s first discourse (Kd 1:6.36.1). Shortly afterwards he inspired Sāriputta with a classic epitome of the teaching (Kd 1:23.2.1).
comment_text.sn22.88:3.10: This is possibly in reference to Godhika, who likewise was distressed by his inability to keep up his meditation (SN 4.23:1.4). States of samādhi are conditioned and dependent on a healthy body, which is why the Bodhisatta needed to eat before he became enlightened (MN 36:33.2). The Buddha urges final Nibbana, which is not a state of consciousness, but freedom from attachment.
comment_text.sn22.88:3.11: The notion that even an arahant can decline is discussed and rejected at Kv 1.2.

root_text.sn22.88:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe.
root_text.sn22.88:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā assaji kassapakārāme viharati ābādhiko dukkhito bāḷhagilāno.
root_text.sn22.88:1.3: Atha kho āyasmā assaji upaṭṭhāke āmantesi:
root_text.sn22.88:1.4: “etha tumhe, āvuso, yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamatha; upasaṅkamitvā mama vacanena bhagavato pāde sirasā vandatha:
root_text.sn22.88:1.8: ‘sādhu kira, bhante, bhagavā yena assaji bhikkhu tenupasaṅkamatu anukampaṁ upādāyā’”ti.
root_text.sn22.88:1.9: “Evamāvuso”ti kho te bhikkhū āyasmato assajissa paṭissutvā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho te bhikkhū bhagavantaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.sn22.88:1.11: sādhu kira, bhante, bhagavā yena assaji bhikkhu tenupasaṅkamatu anukampaṁ upādāyā”ti.
root_text.sn22.88:2.1: Atha kho bhagavā sāyanhasamayaṁ paṭisallānā vuṭṭhito yenāyasmā assaji tenupasaṅkami.
root_text.sn22.88:2.2: Addasā kho āyasmā assaji bhagavantaṁ dūratova āgacchantaṁ.
root_text.sn22.88:2.4: Atha kho bhagavā āyasmantaṁ assajiṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn22.88:2.7: Nisīdi bhagavā paññatte āsane. Nisajja kho bhagavā āyasmantaṁ assajiṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn22.88:2.8: “kacci te, assaji, khamanīyaṁ, kacci yāpanīyaṁ …pe… paṭikkamosānaṁ paññāyati no abhikkamo”ti?
root_text.sn22.88:3.1: “Na me, bhante, khamanīyaṁ …pe… abhikkamosānaṁ paññāyati no paṭikkamo”ti.
root_text.sn22.88:3.2: “Kacci te, assaji, na kiñci kukkuccaṁ na koci vippaṭisāro”ti?
root_text.sn22.88:3.3: “Taggha me, bhante, anappakaṁ kukkuccaṁ anappako vippaṭisāro”ti.
root_text.sn22.88:3.4: “Kacci pana taṁ, assaji, attā sīlato na upavadatī”ti?
root_text.sn22.88:3.5: “Na kho maṁ, bhante, attā sīlato upavadatī”ti.
root_text.sn22.88:3.6: “No ce kira taṁ, assaji, attā sīlato upavadati, atha kiñca te kukkuccaṁ ko ca vippaṭisāro”ti?
root_text.sn22.88:3.7: “Pubbe khvāhaṁ, bhante, gelaññe passambhetvā passambhetvā kāyasaṅkhāre viharāmi, sohaṁ samādhiṁ nappaṭilabhāmi.
root_text.sn22.88:3.8: Tassa mayhaṁ, bhante, taṁ samādhiṁ appaṭilabhato evaṁ hoti:
root_text.sn22.88:3.10: “Ye te, assaji, samaṇabrāhmaṇā samādhisārakā samādhisāmaññā tesaṁ taṁ samādhiṁ appaṭilabhataṁ evaṁ hoti:
root_text.sn22.88:4.8: So sukhañce vedanaṁ vedayati, sā ‘aniccā’ti pajānāti. ‘Anajjhositā’ti pajānāti. ‘Anabhinanditā’ti pajānāti.
root_text.sn22.88:4.9: Dukkhañce vedanaṁ vedayati, sā ‘aniccā’ti pajānāti. ‘Anajjhositā’ti pajānāti. ‘Anabhinanditā’ti pajānāti.
root_text.sn22.88:4.10: Adukkhamasukhañce vedanaṁ vedayati, sā ‘aniccā’ti pajānāti …pe… ‘anabhinanditā’ti pajānāti.
root_text.sn22.88:4.11: So sukhañce vedanaṁ vedayati, visaṁyutto naṁ vedayati;
root_text.sn22.88:4.12: dukkhañce vedanaṁ vedayati, visaṁyutto naṁ vedayati;
root_text.sn22.88:4.13: adukkhamasukhañce vedanaṁ vedayati, visaṁyutto naṁ vedayati.
root_text.sn22.88:4.14: So kāyapariyantikañce vedanaṁ vedayamāno ‘kāyapariyantikaṁ vedanaṁ vedayāmī’ti pajānāti. Jīvitapariyantikañce vedanaṁ vedayamāno ‘jīvitapariyantikaṁ vedanaṁ vedayāmī’ti pajānāti.
root_text.sn22.88:4.15: ‘Kāyassa bhedā uddhaṁ jīvitapariyādānā idheva sabbavedayitāni anabhinanditāni sītībhavissantī’ti pajānāti.
root_text.sn22.88:5.1: Seyyathāpi, assaji, telañca paṭicca, vaṭṭiñca paṭicca, telappadīpo jhāyeyya;
root_text.sn22.88:5.2: tasseva telassa ca vaṭṭiyā ca pariyādānā anāhāro nibbāyeyya.
root_text.sn22.88:5.3: Evameva kho, assaji, bhikkhu kāyapariyantikaṁ vedanaṁ vedayamāno ‘kāyapariyantikaṁ vedanaṁ vedayāmī’ti pajānāti. Jīvitapariyantikaṁ vedanaṁ vedayamāno ‘jīvitapariyantikaṁ vedanaṁ vedayāmī’ti pajānāti.
root_text.sn22.88:5.4: ‘Kāyassa bhedā uddhaṁ jīvitapariyādānā idheva sabbavedayitāni anabhinanditāni sītībhavissantī’ti pajānātī”ti.

translation_text.sn22.88:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrels’ feeding ground.
translation_text.sn22.88:1.2: Now at that time Venerable Assaji was staying in a monastery built by a Kassapa, and he was sick, suffering, gravely ill.
translation_text.sn22.88:1.4: “Please, reverends, go to the Buddha, and in my name bow with your head to his feet. Say to him:
translation_text.sn22.88:1.5: ‘Sir, the mendicant Assaji is sick, suffering, gravely ill.
translation_text.sn22.88:1.6: He bows with his head to your feet.’
translation_text.sn22.88:1.8: ‘Sir, please go to the mendicant Assaji out of sympathy.’”
translation_text.sn22.88:1.9: “Yes, reverend,” those monks replied. They did as he asked.
translation_text.sn22.88:2.1: Then in the late afternoon, the Buddha came out of retreat and went to Venerable Assaji.
translation_text.sn22.88:2.2: Venerable Assaji saw the Buddha coming off in the distance,
translation_text.sn22.88:2.4: But the Buddha said to him,
translation_text.sn22.88:2.5: “It’s all right, Assaji, don’t get up.
translation_text.sn22.88:2.6: There are some seats spread out, I will sit there.”
translation_text.sn22.88:2.7: He sat on the seat spread out and said,
translation_text.sn22.88:2.8: “I hope you’re keeping well, Assaji; I hope you’re all right. And I hope the pain is fading, not growing, that its fading is evident, not its growing.”
translation_text.sn22.88:3.1: “Sir, I’m not all right, I’m not getting by. My pain is terrible and growing, not fading, its growing is evident, not its fading.”
translation_text.sn22.88:3.2: “I hope you don’t have any remorse or regret?”
translation_text.sn22.88:3.3: “Indeed, sir, I have no little remorse and regret.”
translation_text.sn22.88:3.4: “I hope you have no reason to blame yourself when it comes to ethical conduct?”
translation_text.sn22.88:3.5: “No sir, I have no reason to blame myself when it comes to ethical conduct.”
translation_text.sn22.88:3.6: “In that case, Assaji, why do you have remorse and regret?”
translation_text.sn22.88:3.7: “Sir, before my time of illness I meditated having completely stilled the physical process. But now I can’t get immersion.
translation_text.sn22.88:3.8: Since I can’t get immersion, I think:
translation_text.sn22.88:3.10: “There are, Assaji, ascetics and brahmins for whom samādhi is the essence, equating immersion with the ascetic life. They think:
translation_text.sn22.88:4.7: They understand: ‘… there is nothing further for this place.’
translation_text.sn22.88:4.8: If they feel a pleasant feeling, they understand that it’s impermanent, that they’re not attached to it, and that they don’t relish it.
translation_text.sn22.88:4.9: If they feel a painful feeling, they understand that it’s impermanent, that they’re not attached to it, and that they don’t relish it.
translation_text.sn22.88:4.10: If they feel a neutral feeling, they understand that it’s impermanent, that they’re not attached to it, and that they don’t relish it.
translation_text.sn22.88:4.11: If they feel a pleasant feeling, they feel it detached.
translation_text.sn22.88:4.12: If they feel a painful feeling, they feel it detached.
translation_text.sn22.88:4.13: If they feel a neutral feeling, they feel it detached.
translation_text.sn22.88:4.14: Feeling the end of the body draw close, they understand: ‘I feel the end of the body draw close.’ Feeling the end of life draw close, they understand: ‘I feel the end of life draw close.’
translation_text.sn22.88:4.15: They understand: ‘When my body breaks up and my life is over, everything that’s felt, being no longer relished, will become cool right here.’
translation_text.sn22.88:5.1: Suppose an oil lamp depended on oil and a wick to burn.
translation_text.sn22.88:5.2: As the oil and the wick are used up, it would be extinguished due to not being fed.
translation_text.sn22.88:5.3: In the same way, feeling the end of the body draw close, they understand: ‘I feel the end of the body draw close.’ Feeling the end of life draw close, they understand: ‘I feel the end of life draw close.’
translation_text.sn22.88:5.4: They understand: ‘When my body breaks up and my life is over, everything that’s felt, being no longer relished, will become cool right here.’”
